---
title: "Run your first pipeline"
description: "From raw counts to an integrated object — in about 20 minutes."
order: 0
---

Process a real 3-sample dataset from raw counts to an integrated object. First,
choose **how** you want to run it — the steps adapt to your choice.

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
**New here?** Pick **Local** and follow every step top to bottom.
**Already have compute?** Pick **Seqera** or **Viash Hub** — no install needed.
:::

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Local (Nextflow)

**1 · Install the runtime.** You need Nextflow and Docker. Everything else ships
inside containers — you install nothing per pipeline.

```bash
# install nextflow
curl -s https://get.nextflow.io | bash
# check docker is running
docker --version
```

**2 · Get the example data.** A tiny public dataset — three PBMC samples, already
in MuData format.

```bash
aws s3 cp s3://openpipelines-data/pbmc_1k_protein_v3/ ./data --recursive
```

**3 · Run `process_samples`.**

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/multiomics/process_samples/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml --publish_dir results/
```

## Seqera

**1 · Connect a compute environment.** No local install. In Seqera Platform, set
up a compute environment (AWS / Azure / GCP) once — every run reuses it.

**2 · Get the example data.** Point at the same public S3 dataset; Seqera reads
it directly: `s3://openpipelines-data/pbmc_1k_protein_v3/`.

**3 · Launch with `tw`** (or the Launchpad web form):

```bash
tw launch openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  --revision 4.2.0 --pull-latest \
  --main-script .../workflows/multiomics/process_samples/main.nf \
  --compute-env <your-env-id> --params-file params.yaml
```

## Viash Hub

**1 · Open the workflow.** No install, no CLI. Find `openpipeline` in the
[Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com) catalog and open `process_samples`.

**2 · Fill in the form.** The GUI renders every parameter with its description —
set your input and thresholds, no YAML required.

**3 · Pick compute & run.** Choose local execution or a connected Seqera
environment, then press **Launch**.

:::

## One run, many samples — the `param_list`

To process a whole cohort in a single command, pass a **`param_list`**: a list
where **each entry is one sample** with its own arguments (`id`, `input`, and any
per-sample thresholds). Arguments you set **outside** the list apply to **every**
sample. Shared settings stay global; per-sample overrides live next to their
sample — no scripting, no repeated commands.

```yaml
# params.yaml — two samples, per-sample filter + a shared setting
param_list:
  - id: sample_1
    input: data/sample_1.h5mu
    rna_min_counts: 2          # only for sample_1
  - id: sample_2
    input: data/sample_2.h5mu
    rna_min_counts: 1          # only for sample_2
rna_max_counts: 1000000        # applied to every sample
```

## Read the output

The result is one `.h5mu` file:

```
MuData
 ├─ .mod
 │   └─ rna                  # filtered, normalized, log1p
 │       ├─ .X               # normalized counts
 │       ├─ .obsm["X_pca"]   # dimensionality reduction
 │       └─ .obs["leiden"]   # cell clusters
 └─ .obs["sample_id"]        # which sample each cell came from
```

**Next:** [task guides →](../guides/index.qmd) · [every parameter →](../reference/index.qmd)
